definition: The stagflationary vs. disinflationary outcome bifurcation is the dominant portfolio construction variable. Under stagflation (tariff escalation, supply shocks), stock-bond correlation turns positive, destroying 60/40 portfolio hedging. Under disinflation (demand weakness), correlation stays negative and bonds hedge. The tariff/de-globalization trajectory is the key swing variable.
evidence: 5Y5Y breakevens at 2.3% sit at the threshold between regimes
Stock-bond correlation regime determines 60/40 portfolio efficacy
Framework is analytically sound per debates though probability weights were refuted
open_questions: What are defensible probability weights for stagflationary vs disinflationary scenarios?
How should portfolios be positioned for correlation regime uncertainty?
